title: Media, Connectors, Transceivers, and Architectures · Incident Case: Net Topologies
summary: Select and document a campus and data-center physical design under distance, bandwidth, environment, and redundancy constraints. Planned artifact: Add and verify the net topologies increment in a physical media and topology bill of design. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Compare mesh, hybrid, star, hub-and-spoke, point-to-point, and failure effects.
Select Ethernet or Fibre Channel SFP and QSFP form factors by protocol, wavelength, fiber, distance, lane, and device support.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# comptia-network-plus safe operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic browser simulator.
# Add one observation, command, expected signal, and conclusion per activity.

steps.title: Build Net Topologies evidence
steps.instruction: Append a safe investigation headed "# Evidence: net-topologies-pr4xzz" with a concrete hypothesis, one read-only network command, and an observation that changes the next decision.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Net Topologies into observable performance evidence.

steps.title: Defend the Net Topologies model
